actions.narration: Okay, landing on the tasks page. It says 'Welcome back!' and 'Successfully logged in to Survey Corps.' which is...weird, since I haven't logged in yet. That's a bit confusing right off the bat. There's a navigation bar at the top, options for 'Tasks,' 'Calendar View,' 'Dashboard,' 'Tags,' and 'Theme'. Then a bunch of options for viewing tasks: 'List View,' 'Canvas View,' 'Daily,' 'Weekly,' 'Monthly,' 'Yearly.' And then some filters: 'Categories,' 'Priorities,' 'Deadline,' 'Status.' Seems pretty comprehensive, which is good, but also a little overwhelming at first glance.

Alright, I need to find a login or registration button first. 'Edit Profile' and 'Sign Out' are at the top, which implies I'm already logged in, despite what I think. Since I'm supposed to register first, I'm assuming I'll have to log out first. Let me click 'Sign Out'.

Okay, I'm probably back on the main page now. There should be a way to register here. Since this page URL specified it, I'm guessing I have to poke around myself to find it. This is not a great user experience at all. There aren't any clear calls to action.
I've clicked around for a while and I can't find a registration form at all. I'm guessing the task to register is impossible to complete without the feature even existing on the page.

friction_points: Application claims user is logged in on initial page load when they are not.
No clear registration or login options presented after 'Sign Out'.
Missing registration functionality to complete the task list.
